title: Corybas ponapensis
taxonomy: Corybas ponapensis is a species of orchid in the genus Corybas. Corybas ponapensis belongs to the family Diaphantheae, which is a subtribe within the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Corybas ponapensis is further classified as a type of slipper orchid, specifically a terrestrial species. It is native to the island of Pohnpei in Micronesia. This species is a relatively rare and unique member of the orchid family.

intro: Corybas ponapensis, also known as Ponape Island helmet orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid endemic to the island of Pohnpei in the Federated States of Micronesia. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Corybas ponapensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Corybas ponapensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as a tincture, to take advantage of its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
